LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ASTRAZENECA PHARMACEUTICALS LP AND ASTRAZENECA, LP'S MOTION TO DISMISS BASED ON FORUM NON-CONVENIENS IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA IS A SERIOUSLY INCONVENIENT FORUM, TENNESSEE IS A SUITABLE ALTERNATIVE FORUM, AND THE PUBLIC AND PRIVATE FACTORS FAVOR HAVING THIS CASE HEARD IN TENNESSEE RATHER THAN CALIFORNIA. ONE ORDER INCORPORATING ALL MOTIONS AND JOINDERS HEARD THIS DAY SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-02-2012 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Based On Forum Non-Conveniens; Consent To Jurisdiction; Compendium Of Non-California Authorioties; Memo Of P&A; Declaration
Off Calendar Nov-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ASTRAZENECA PHARMACEUTICALS LP AND ASTRAZENECA, LP'S MOTION TO DISMISS BASED ON FORUM NON-CONVENIENS.
Appearances: William E. Steimle of King & Spalding (318.1259) for defendants and moving parties. No other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. California is a seriously inconvenient forum, Tennessee is a suitable alternative forum, and the public and private factors favor having this case heard in Tennessee rather than California. One order incorporating all motions and joinders heard this day signed in open court. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288
